docs.title: Welcome to The Great CCCB
docs.text: This is my home page right?

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Admin Workflow
docs.text:
This document explains the workflow for maintaining and publishing the Community Ground Knowledge Base using:

  • Obsidian
  • MkDocs
  • GitHub
  • GitHub Pages

docs.title: System Overview
docs.text:
The system works like this:

Obsidian → Markdown Files → GitHub → MkDocs → Public Website

All content is stored as standard markdown (.md) files.

These files are: - edited locally in Obsidian - backed up in GitHub - automatically published as a website using MkDocs

docs.title: Folder Structure
docs.text:
Project folder:

community-ground/
├── docs/
├── mkdocs.yml

The docs/ folder contains all website content.

This same folder is also opened as the Obsidian vault.

Example structure:

docs/
├── index.md
├── admin-workflow.md
├── recipes/
├── stories/
├── maintenance/
├── events/
├── photos/

docs.title: Opening the Vault in Obsidian
docs.text:
Open Obsidian and choose:

Open folder as vault

Select:

C:\Users\Jacob\community-ground\docs

Do NOT select the entire community-ground folder.

Only select the docs folder.

docs.title: Running the Website Locally
docs.text:
Open PowerShell:

cd C:\Users\Jacob\community-ground
python -m mkdocs serve

Then open:

http://127.0.0.1:8000

This creates a live preview of the website.

Any changes saved in Obsidian will automatically appear in the local website preview.

docs.title: Creating New Pages
docs.text:
Inside Obsidian: - create a new note - place it in the correct folder - save normally

Example:

recipes/damper.md

docs.title: Recommended Markdown Style
docs.text:
Example page:

# Damper Recipe

## Ingredients
- Flour
- Water
- Salt

## Method
Mix ingredients and cook over fire.

docs.title: Internal Linking
docs.text:
Use Obsidian wiki-links:

[[Damper Recipe]]
[[Kitchen Procedures]]

This allows notes to connect together like a wiki.

docs.title: Images
docs.text:
Store images inside a folder such as:

docs/photos/

Example markdown image:

![Kitchen Setup](photos/kitchen.jpg)

docs.title: Website Navigation
docs.text:
Website navigation is controlled by:

mkdocs.yml

If a new page is added, it should also be added to navigation.

Example:

nav:
  - Home: index.md

  - Recipes:
      - Damper Recipe: recipes/damper.md

  - Stories:
      - Community History: stories/history.md

  - Admin:
      - Workflow: admin-workflow.md

docs.title: Publishing Changes
docs.text:
After making changes in Obsidian:

Open PowerShell:

cd C:\Users\Jacob\community-ground

Then run:

git add .
git commit -m "Updated content"
git push
python -m mkdocs gh-deploy

This: 1. saves changes into Git history 2. uploads changes to GitHub 3. republishes the website

docs.title: Website URL
docs.text:
Published website:

https://YOURUSERNAME.github.io/community-ground/

Replace YOURUSERNAME with the GitHub username.

docs.title: Daily Workflow
docs.text:
Typical workflow:

  1. Open Obsidian
  2. Edit or create notes
  3. Preview locally using MkDocs
  4. Commit changes to GitHub
  5. Deploy website

docs.title: Always work inside the project folder
docs.text:
Before using Git commands:

cd C:\Users\Jacob\community-ground

docs.title: Keep filenames simple
docs.text:
Good:

damper-recipe.md

Avoid: - spaces - special characters - very long names

docs.title: Use folders early
docs.text:
Keeping content organised early prevents future mess.

Recommended categories: - recipes - stories - maintenance - events - admin - photos

docs.title: Recommended Future Improvements
docs.text:
Possible future additions:

  • Search improvements
  • Tags/categories
  • User contribution guides
  • Custom domain
  • Photo galleries
  • Comments
  • Automated deployment
  • Obsidian Git integration

docs.title: Backup Safety
docs.text:
GitHub acts as: - backup - version history - change tracking

If something breaks: - previous versions can be restored

docs.title: Philosophy
docs.text:
The goal of this system is:

  • simple
  • free
  • future-proof
  • easy to edit
  • community-owned
  • portable

All content remains as plain markdown files that can be moved anywhere in the future.
